title: WHO Update: MV Hondius Hantavirus Cluster — 5 Confirmed Cases, 3 Deaths
excerpt: The World Health Organization has updated its assessment of the hantavirus cluster linked to the MV Hondius expedition vessel. As of 7 May 2026, a total of 8 cases have been reported: 5 laboratory-confirmed and 3 suspected. Three deaths have been recorded among the confirmed cases.
content:
The World Health Organization (WHO) published an updated assessment on 7 May 2026 regarding the hantavirus cluster linked to the MV Hondius expedition vessel.

Key figures (as of 7 May 2026):
• Total reported cases: 8
• Laboratory-confirmed hantavirus cases: 5
• Suspected cases: 3
• Deaths: 3

This represents an increase from the initial Disease Outbreak News report dated 4 May 2026, which reported 7 total cases (2 confirmed, 5 suspected, 3 deaths). The revision reflects the completion of laboratory testing that reclassified 3 previously suspected cases as confirmed.

WHO continues to monitor the situation and coordinate with national health authorities of affected countries. Contact tracing of all passengers and crew aboard the MV Hondius is ongoing.

Note: All data in this report is sourced directly from WHO official communications. HantaWorld does not independently verify case counts — all figures are attributed to their original source.

title: WHO Disease Outbreak News: Hantavirus — MV Hondius Multi-Country Cluster
excerpt: On 4 May 2026, WHO published a Disease Outbreak News (DON) report regarding a hantavirus cluster associated with the MV Hondius expedition vessel. At that time, 7 cases had been reported: 2 confirmed and 5 suspected, with 3 deaths recorded.
content:
The World Health Organization (WHO) issued a Disease Outbreak News (DON) on 4 May 2026 concerning a cluster of hantavirus cases linked to the MV Hondius, an expedition vessel.

Key figures (as of 4 May 2026):
• Total reported cases: 7
• Laboratory-confirmed hantavirus cases: 2
• Suspected cases: 5
• Deaths: 3

The cases were reported across multiple nationalities, with initial exposure believed to be associated with the expedition's itinerary. National health authorities in affected countries were notified and contact tracing operations were initiated.

Note: This is a historical timeline entry. Updated figures are available in the WHO update dated 7 May 2026, which reports 8 total cases (5 confirmed, 3 suspected, 3 deaths).

All data in this report is sourced directly from WHO Disease Outbreak News. HantaWorld does not independently verify case counts — all figures are attributed to their original source.
